claim: Parallel agent invocations that mutate tracked repository files shall use git worktree isolation unless their target files are provably disjoint and no history-rewriting git operation is planned during their execution window.
why: Incident 2026-06-30/07-01: two parallel agents (P5-noise-fix and enforceability-flag tasks) both touched overlapping graph and invariants territory in one shared working tree. A subsequent git filter-repo hard-reset (run by the director to purge .idea/__pycache__ from committed history) wiped one agent's uncommitted work because it had not yet been committed and was not isolated in a worktree. The fix had to be redone from scratch. This requirement crystallizes the lesson: parallel mutating agents belong in isolated worktrees, or the director must guarantee no history-rewrite runs while their work is uncommitted. Not yet implemented as an enforced mechanism -- no spawn-log / worktree-isolation-status tooling exists (the spawn-log-isolation-status tool is Planned, not Implemented). The discipline is INHERENTLY_PROSE: it is a fact about an operating procedure (parallel agent orchestration), not a property of the committed graph that a check_* can verify. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
